title: Leymus ovatus

intro: Leymus ovatus, also known as the beaked meadow grass or Leymus, is a perennial grass native to temperate regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive beaked spikelets. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues, such as bloating and indigestion, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Leymus ovatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Leymus ovatus is often prepared as a tea or dried powder and used to treat digestive issues, with common preparations including infusions and decoctions.
